signals.idea:
Sync Discord `_resources` channel posts into banodoco-website as auto-published resources, with a "Made with this" section containing reply media and a Discord-style comments thread.

# Context

Single shared Supabase project (`ujlwuvkrxlvoswwkerdf`) backs both brain-of-bndc (Discord archive bot, Python, Railway) and banodoco-website (React/Vite). Discord resource posts live in 5 forum channels: `resources` (1149372684220768367), `flux_resources` (1275200992136400967), `wan_resources` (1373291419434877078), `ltx_resources` (1457981813120176138), `acestep_resources` (1472633200491626526). Each is a forum, so every message has a `thread_id`; an OP is where `message_id == thread_id`. Archive pipeline already writes every message + reaction into `discord_messages`. Current volume: ~15 qualifying OPs in the last 30 days.

# Trigger + scope

- Thread OP in any of the 5 `_resources` channels with `reaction_count >= 5` (any emoji counts) becomes an `assets` row.
- Backfill: last 60 days on first run, chronological oldest→newest, resumable via per-channel cursor.
- Ongoing: polled every 10 min.
- No un-listing if reactions later drop.
- Auto-publish `admin_status='Listed'`; admin kill switch via `assets.is_hidden`.
- Each reply in the thread becomes an `asset_comments` row. Reply attachments become `media` rows linked via `asset_comment_media`. OP attachments go via existing `asset_media`.

# Schema deltas

Extend `assets`:
- `source` TEXT ('manual' | 'discord_import')
- `discord_thread_id` BIGINT UNIQUE NULL
- `discord_channel_id` BIGINT NULL
- `imported_at` TIMESTAMPTZ
- `last_synced_at` TIMESTAMPTZ
- `reactions_reached_threshold_at` TIMESTAMPTZ
- `is_hidden` BOOLEAN DEFAULT FALSE

New `asset_comments`:
- `id` UUID PK
- `asset_id` UUID FK → assets(id) ON DELETE CASCADE
- `discord_message_id` BIGINT UNIQUE NOT NULL
- `discord_thread_id` BIGINT NOT NULL
- `author_member_id` BIGINT FK → discord_members(member_id)
- `content` TEXT
- `reply_to_comment_id` UUID FK → asset_comments(id) NULL (resolves from Discord `reference_id`)
- `reply_to_discord_message_id` BIGINT NULL (denormalized for resolvability even when the parent isn't imported)
- `reaction_count` INT DEFAULT 0
- `discord_created_at` TIMESTAMPTZ
- `discord_edited_at` TIMESTAMPTZ
- `is_deleted` BOOLEAN DEFAULT FALSE
- `created_at`, `updated_at` TIMESTAMPTZ

New `asset_comment_media`:
- `comment_id` UUID FK → asset_comments(id) ON DELETE CASCADE
- `media_id` UUID FK → media(id) ON DELETE CASCADE
- `sort_order` INT DEFAULT 0
- PK (comment_id, media_id)

`media` changes:
- Add `'discord-comment'` as a valid `classification` value
- Provenance stored in `media.metadata` JSONB: `{discord_message_id, discord_channel_id, discord_attachment_id, original_cdn_url, imported_at}`
- Dedup by `discord_attachment_id` (query `metadata->>'discord_attachment_id'`)

New `media_import_jobs` queue:
- `id` UUID PK
- `discord_attachment_id` BIGINT UNIQUE
- `discord_message_id` BIGINT
- `target_kind` TEXT ('asset_media' | 'asset_comment_media')
- `target_id` UUID (asset_id or comment_id depending on target_kind)
- `original_cdn_url` TEXT
- `filename` TEXT, `content_type` TEXT, `size_bytes` BIGINT
- `status` TEXT ('pending' | 'in_progress' | 'done' | 'failed' | 'skipped')
- `attempts` INT DEFAULT 0
- `last_error` TEXT
- `media_id` UUID FK → media(id) NULL (set on done)
- `created_at`, `updated_at`, `locked_until` TIMESTAMPTZ

Add indexes for: `assets.discord_thread_id`, `asset_comments(asset_id, discord_created_at)`, `asset_comments.discord_message_id`, `media_import_jobs(status, locked_until)`.

RLS on new tables: public read for `asset_comments` / `asset_comment_media` (matches `asset_media`); service-role only for `media_import_jobs`.

# Runtime

Two Supabase Edge Functions + pg_cron:

**`discord-resource-promoter`** (cron: every 10 min; ~30s wall clock):
1. For each of the 5 channels, advance the per-channel cursor (`discord_resource_sync_cursors` table: `channel_id`, `last_processed_created_at`, `last_backfill_cursor_at`, `backfill_complete` BOOL).
2. Discover qualifying thread OPs: `discord_messages` where `channel_id IN (...)`, `message_id = thread_id`, `reaction_count >= 5`, `created_at > cursor`.
3. Upsert `assets` rows keyed on `discord_thread_id`. Populate: `name` (first line of content, truncated), `description` (full content), `type` (infer via channel → 'lora' | 'tool'; default 'tool'), `member_id` (from author), `admin_status='Listed'`, `source='discord_import'`, `reactions_reached_threshold_at=NOW()`.
4. For each imported thread, fetch all replies: `discord_messages WHERE thread_id = X AND message_id != thread_id`. Upsert `asset_comments` keyed on `discord_message_id`. Resolve `reply_to_comment_id` from Discord `reference_id` where possible.
5. For every attachment (OP and replies), upsert a `media_import_jobs` row if not already present. Use Discord `attachment.id` as dedup key.
6. Update `assets.last_synced_at`.
7. Paginate/time-slice to stay under 30s budget; advance cursor even on partial progress.

**`discord-media-importer`** (cron: every 2 min; up to 400s):
1. Claim up to N pending jobs via `UPDATE ... SET status='in_progress', locked_until=NOW()+interval '10m' WHERE status='pending' AND (locked_until IS NULL OR locked_until < NOW()) ... RETURNING *`. Re-claim stuck `in_progress` past `locked_until`.
2. For each job:
   - Refresh Discord CDN URL (use bot token via Discord API `GET /channels/{channel_id}/messages/{message_id}` to get current signed URL — mirror logic from existing `refresh-media-urls` edge function).
   - Stream download from Discord CDN.
   - If video/gif: stream upload to Cloudflare Stream via tus; poll until `readyToStream`; capture `playback.hls`, `thumbnail`, `duration`.
   - If image: stream upload to Supabase Storage `user-uploads/discord-imports/{discord_message_id}/{attachment_id}-{filename}`; generate thumbnail via image transform if available.
   - Other (zip/json): upload to same bucket, no thumbnail.
   - Insert `media` row: `classification='discord-comment'` (or `'post'` for OP attachments — decide convention in plan), `member_id` from message author, Cloudflare/storage URLs populated, `metadata` with Discord provenance.
   - Insert junction row (`asset_media` or `asset_comment_media` based on `target_kind`).
   - Mark job `done`, set `media_id`.
3. On failure: increment `attempts`, write `last_error`, status back to `pending` with exponential backoff via `locked_until`. Skip permanently after 5 attempts (`status='failed'`).

Secrets required on Supabase Edge Function config:
- `DISCORD_BOT_TOKEN`
- `CLOUDFLARE_STREAM_ACCOUNT_ID`
- `CLOUDFLARE_STREAM_API_TOKEN`
- Existing `SUPABASE_URL` / `SUPABASE_SERVICE_ROLE_KEY` implicit

# Frontend

Update `banodoco-website/src/pages/Resources/ResourceModal.tsx`:
1. Keep existing gallery strip (primary media + `asset_media`).
2. Add **"Made with this"** section below gallery — grid of all `asset_comment_media` thumbnails for the asset, newest first, click opens lightbox anchored to the parent comment.
3. Add **Discussion** section below — flat chronological list of `asset_comments`. Each row:
   - Author chip (avatar + global_name from `discord_members`) + timestamp
   - "Replying to @name" chip with quoted excerpt if `reply_to_comment_id` set
   - Markdown-rendered `content`
   - Attached media grid (inline video/image thumbs, click → lightbox)
   - Reaction count chip
   - "View on Discord" link (build URL from channel_id + message_id)
4. New hook `useAssetComments(assetId)` — single query joining `asset_comments` + `asset_comment_media` + `media` + `discord_members`, ordered by `discord_created_at`.
5. Resource card: small "From Discord" badge (channel name) on imported assets — subtle, not a big label.

# Observability + ops

- `media_import_jobs` table is the operational dashboard (count by status, oldest pending, failures with `last_error`).
- Errors logged to `system_logs` (existing infrastructure).
- Health check query: failed jobs in last 24h, stuck `in_progress` past lock, per-channel cursor age.
- Dry-run mode: env var `DRY_RUN=true` on promoter → logs would-be inserts without writing.

# Idempotency + edge cases to handle in plan

- Discord message edits: re-sync updates `asset_comments.content`, `discord_edited_at`.
- Discord message deletions: `discord_messages.is_deleted=true` → set `asset_comments.is_deleted=true` (UI hides).
- Attachment removal on edit: don't delete `media` (preserves history); could mark `is_deleted` on junction.
- Race: promoter tries to insert comment before OP asset exists — only process replies for threads whose asset row already exists.
- Rate limits: Discord API 50 req/s; space attachment URL refreshes.
- Cloudflare Stream failures: retry; skip after N attempts.
- `discord_members` might not yet have the author when a comment arrives — promoter should upsert a thin member row if missing (or the existing archive sync covers this — verify).
- Already-imported content: must be fully idempotent — reruns should produce zero new rows.

# Out of scope (do NOT include)

- Live-update (websocket) — polling is fine.
- Moderation UI beyond the `is_hidden` flag (no approval queue, no review surface).
- Cross-posting from website back to Discord.
- User-initiated imports ("turn this Discord post into a resource" button).
- Migrating existing manually-created `assets` rows — they stay `source='manual'`.
- Changing the archive pipeline on brain-of-bndc (the existing archive covers everything the promoter needs).

# Deliverables megaplan should produce

1. Ordered implementation phases with a clear first-working-version milestone (schema + promoter dry-run) before media rehosting is wired up.
2. Migration SQL file list (timestamped, in `/Users/peteromalley/Documents/banodoco-workspace/supabase/migrations/`).
3. Edge function directory structure under `supabase/functions/` (promoter, importer, shared helpers — there's already a `supabase/functions/_shared/` in the workspace).
4. Hook + component additions in `banodoco-website/src/` with concrete file paths.
5. pg_cron SQL snippets to register the two scheduled invocations.
6. Test/verification steps per phase (e.g. one-channel dry run, then rehost a single attachment, then full backfill).
7. Rollback plan per phase.
8. Explicit risk register (Cloudflare quota, Discord rate limits, edge function timeout, storage cost on 60-day backfill).

unresolved_flags.concern: Discord resource promotion: late-threshold coverage is still incomplete because the revised rolling 60-day scan misses threads older than 60 days that reach the reaction threshold later.
Discord media sync infrastructure: the revised Cloudflare/vault reuse story is still incomplete against the actual repository contracts.

Are the proposed changes technically correct?: The replacement for FLAG-001 is still incomplete. V2 no longer uses `created_at > cursor`, but it still limits discovery to `discord_messages.created_at > NOW() - INTERVAL '60 days'`; a thread created 61+ days ago that only later reaches 5 reactions would still never import, even though the product rule says any qualifying OP becomes an asset and the ongoing sync is not scoped to recent posts only.

Are the proposed changes technically correct?: The plan’s failure-handling story for missing members is not technically sufficient in SQL form. It says an FK miss will be 'logged and skipped', but the proposed promoter logic is a set-based `INSERT ... SELECT` inside one PL/pgSQL function; if one row violates the `members(member_id)` FK, PostgreSQL aborts the statement unless the plan introduces row-level exception isolation, so one bad author can block the whole promoter run rather than just one asset/comment.

Are the proposed changes technically correct?: The proposed asset type mapping (`lora` or `tool`) does not match the website’s current resource model. Manual resource creation only offers `lora` and `workflow` (`banodoco-website/src/pages/SubmitResource/index.tsx:10-13`), and the cards/filters branch on `asset.type === 'workflow'` (`ResourceModal.tsx:151-156`, `ResourceCard.tsx:151-154`, `useResourceFilters.ts:159-160`). Importing Discord rows with `type='tool'` would render them as LoRAs in some places and make them unfilterable in others.

Does the change touch all locations AND supporting infrastructure?: The Cloudflare reuse story is still incomplete against the actual repository code. `supabase/functions/cloudflare-stream-webhook/index.ts:210-240` only writes `cloudflare_playback_hls_url`, `cloudflare_thumbnail_url`, and `storage_provider`; it does not persist duration, even though the revised plan and success criteria say the existing webhook will populate HLS, thumbnail, and duration for imported videos. That supporting-infrastructure contract is still inaccurate unless the plan adds a webhook change or drops duration from the promise.

Does the change touch all locations AND supporting infrastructure?: The new vault bootstrap step is not yet self-contained enough to satisfy the plan’s own `supabase db reset` criterion. There is no existing `vault.create_secret`, `internal.get_service_role_key`, or secret-seeding pattern anywhere under `supabase/`, and the proposed migration depends on an out-of-band `current_setting('app.settings.service_role_key_value', true)` value that the repo never sets. As written, the bootstrap is still an infrastructure gap rather than a ready migration recipe.

Does the change touch all locations AND supporting infrastructure?: The cron registration snippet assumes a secret-delivery pattern that is not present elsewhere in the repo. Existing pg_cron usage schedules local SQL directly (`supabase/migrations/20260403000005_schedule_priority_scores_cron.sql:4-23`), and `supabase/config.toml:45-46` only contains a commented `db.vault` stub. A migration that references `current_setting('app.settings.service_role_key')` without first establishing that setting will not be self-contained.

Discord promoter execution: the revised SQL-native promoter still lacks row-level error isolation, so one missing-member FK or other bad row can abort the whole sync instead of being logged and skipped.
Discord resource discussion UI: the revised frontend plan still assumes an existing comment lightbox/navigation surface that does not exist in the current caller path.

Did the work fully address the issue hints, user notes, and approved plan requirements?: The revision diverges from the user’s stated runtime/deliverable shape in a way that still needs to be flagged. The brief asked for two Supabase Edge Functions plus pg_cron, with a promoter function directory under `supabase/functions/`, and a resumable per-channel cursor table; v2 replaces the promoter with PL/pgSQL and removes the cursor table entirely. That may be a pragmatic simplification, but it is still a requirements change rather than a straight implementation of the approved shape.

Search for related code that handles the same concept. Is the reported issue a symptom of something broader?: The discussion/media interaction work is still broader than the plan acknowledges. `ResourceDetail` currently only swaps `activeMedia` inline (`banodoco-website/src/pages/ResourceDetail/index.tsx:59-74, 228-245`) and the resource area has no existing comment lightbox component to reuse; the plan nevertheless says comment thumbnails will open an 'existing lightbox' anchored to the parent comment. That means a non-trivial new modal/state/navigation surface is still missing from the scoped implementation steps.

Find the callers of the changed function. What arguments do they actually pass? Does the fix handle all of them?: I also checked the concrete caller that would need to host the new interaction model. `ResourceDetail` currently has only `activeMedia`/`setActiveMedia` state and no comment-modal or lightbox caller boundary (`banodoco-website/src/pages/ResourceDetail/index.tsx:59-74`), while `rg -n "ResourceModal" banodoco-website/src` still shows no imports/usages for the modal. The plan’s promise of an 'existing lightbox' anchored to a parent comment therefore still lacks a real caller path in the current codebase.

resolved_flags.concern: Discord member fallback: the proposed thin upsert is incompatible with the current members schema because username is still required.
resolved_flags.resolution: Reworked v1 to address all 17 gate-identified defects. Largest structural changes: (1) promoter becomes a PL/pgSQL stored function invoked by pg_cron directly, matching the repo's existing `schedule_priority_scores_cron.sql` pattern and eliminating the edge-function auth problem; (2) discovery uses an anti-join on `assets.discord_thread_id` within a rolling 60-day window instead of a `created_at > cursor` scan, so late-threshold crossings are imported; (3) media importer reuses the existing `cloudflare-stream-webhook` + `cloudflare-stream-backfill` functions (no Cloudflare polling); (4) frontend work moves to `ResourceDetail/index.tsx` (the live surface) with matching changes at `ResourceModal.tsx` to honor the user's scope note; (5) asset `type` mapping fixed to `workflow`/`lora` matching `SubmitResource/index.tsx:10-13`; (6) member fallback removed — trust archive pipeline; (7) `useResources`, `useCommunityResources`, `useCommunityResource`, `useUserProfile` all updated to SELECT + filter on `is_hidden`; (8) `asset_comment_media` + `asset_media` gain `is_deleted` columns for attachment-removal-on-edit; (9) vault bootstrap for service-role key is a first-class migration step; (10) shared `AssetDescription` component applies markdown rendering to both manual and Discord-imported asset descriptions with an explicit success criterion, no custom embed tokens.

resolved_flags.concern: Resource typing: importing assets as `tool` conflicts with the website’s existing `lora`/`workflow` assumptions.
resolved_flags.resolution: Reworked v1 to address all 17 gate-identified defects. Largest structural changes: (1) promoter becomes a PL/pgSQL stored function invoked by pg_cron directly, matching the repo's existing `schedule_priority_scores_cron.sql` pattern and eliminating the edge-function auth problem; (2) discovery uses an anti-join on `assets.discord_thread_id` within a rolling 60-day window instead of a `created_at > cursor` scan, so late-threshold crossings are imported; (3) media importer reuses the existing `cloudflare-stream-webhook` + `cloudflare-stream-backfill` functions (no Cloudflare polling); (4) frontend work moves to `ResourceDetail/index.tsx` (the live surface) with matching changes at `ResourceModal.tsx` to honor the user's scope note; (5) asset `type` mapping fixed to `workflow`/`lora` matching `SubmitResource/index.tsx:10-13`; (6) member fallback removed — trust archive pipeline; (7) `useResources`, `useCommunityResources`, `useCommunityResource`, `useUserProfile` all updated to SELECT + filter on `is_hidden`; (8) `asset_comment_media` + `asset_media` gain `is_deleted` columns for attachment-removal-on-edit; (9) vault bootstrap for service-role key is a first-class migration step; (10) shared `AssetDescription` component applies markdown rendering to both manual and Discord-imported asset descriptions with an explicit success criterion, no custom embed tokens.

resolved_flags.concern: Resource frontend surface: the plan targets an effectively unused modal and does not cover the route-driven ResourceDetail page that users actually hit.

resolved_flags.concern: Did the work fully address the issue hints, user notes, and approved plan requirements?: The user’s added scope note about markdown-rendering resource descriptions is only partially reflected in the plan. The repo does have the exact plain-text `ResourceModal` block the note calls out at `banodoco-website/src/pages/Resources/ResourceModal.tsx:186-189`, but the plan never adds a concrete step for that rendering change or the requested success criterion; it only mentions using the existing markdown renderer for comment content.

Largest structural changes: (1) promoter becomes a PL/pgSQL stored function invoked by pg_cron directly, matching the repo's existing `schedule_priority_scores_cron.sql` pattern and eliminating the edge-function auth problem; (2) discovery uses an anti-join on `assets.discord_thread_id` within a rolling 60-day window instead of a `created_at > cursor` scan, so late-threshold crossings are imported; (3) media importer reuses the existing `cloudflare-stream-webhook` + `cloudflare-stream-backfill` functions (no Cloudflare polling); (4) frontend work moves to `ResourceDetail/index.tsx` (the live surface) with matching changes at `ResourceModal.tsx` to honor the user's scope note; (5) asset `type` mapping fixed to `workflow`/`lora` matching `SubmitResource/index.tsx:10-13`; (6) member fallback removed — trust archive pipeline; (7) `useResources`, `useCommunityResources`, `useCommunityResource`, `useUserProfile` all updated to SELECT + filter on `is_hidden`; (8) `asset_comment_media` + `asset_media` gain `is_deleted` columns for attachment-removal-on-edit; (9) vault bootstrap for service-role key is a first-class migration step; (10) shared `AssetDescription` component applies markdown rendering to both manual and Discord-imported asset descriptions with an explicit success criterion, no custom embed tokens.

resolved_flags.concern: Did the work fully address the issue hints, user notes, and approved plan requirements?: One of the explicit edge cases in the brief, attachment removal on Discord edit, is still unresolved in the plan. The proposed schema for `asset_comment_media` only has `(comment_id, media_id, sort_order)` and no visibility/deleted flag, so the plan’s note that it 'could mark `is_deleted` on junction' is not actually backed by any migration or follow-up step.

resolved_flags.concern: Search for related code that handles the same concept. Is the reported issue a symptom of something broader?: The plan scopes the frontend work around `ResourceModal`, but `rg -n "ResourceModal" banodoco-website/src` only finds the component definition and no callers. The live resource surface is `banodoco-website/src/pages/ResourceDetail/index.tsx`, which still renders `resource.description` as plain text at lines 166-169 and already owns the visible 'Made with this' gallery. Limiting the change to the dead modal leaves the actual resource page behavior unchanged.

resolved_flags.concern: Search for related code that handles the same concept. Is the reported issue a symptom of something broader?: The broader 'resource visibility' surface is larger than the plan accounts for. Resource lists and profile counts currently come from `useResources.ts`, `useCommunityResources.ts`, `useCommunityResource.ts`, and `useUserProfile.ts`, all of which filter only on `admin_status` and never consider a future `assets.is_hidden` kill switch (`useResources.ts:51-74`, `useCommunityResources.ts:118-127`, `useCommunityResource.ts:103-113`, `useUserProfile.ts:125-133`). If `is_hidden` is meant to hide imported assets globally, the change needs to reach those callers too, not just one modal/card.
resolved_flags.resolution: Reworked v1 to address all 17 gate-identified defects. Largest structural changes: (1) promoter becomes a PL/pgSQL stored function invoked by pg_cron directly, matching the repo's existing `schedule_priority_scores_cron.sql` pattern and eliminating the edge-function auth problem; (2) discovery uses an anti-join on `assets.discord_thread_id` within a rolling 60-day window instead of a `created_at > cursor` scan, so late-threshold crossings are imported; (3) media importer reuses the existing `cloudflare-stream-webhook` + `cloudflare-stream-backfill` functions (no Cloudflare polling); (4) frontend work moves to `ResourceDetail/index.tsx` (the live surface) with matching changes at `ResourceModal.tsx` to honor the user's scope note; (5) asset `type` mapping fixed to `workflow`/`lora` matching `SubmitResource/index.tsx:10-13`; (6) member fallback removed — trust archive pipeline; (7) `useResources`, `useCommunityResources`, `useCommunityResource`, `useUserProfile` all updated to SELECT + filter on `is_hidden`; (8) `asset_comment_media` + `asset_media` gain `is_deleted` columns for attachment-removal-on-edit; (9) vault bootstrap for service-role key is a first-class migration step; (10) shared `AssetDescription` component applies markdown rendering to both manual and Discord-imported asset descriptions with an explicit success criterion, no custom embed tokens.

resolved_flags.concern: Find the callers of the changed function. What arguments do they actually pass? Does the fix handle all of them?: I traced the current callers that consume resource rows, and they all pass through the existing hooks with narrow assumptions about the payload: `useResources.ts` reads `id, type, name, description, admin_status, ...`, `useCommunityResources.ts` reads `id, name, description, type, lora_link, ...`, and `useCommunityResource.ts` reads `id, name, description, type, ...` (`useResources.ts:51-65`, `useCommunityResources.ts:118-124`, `useCommunityResource.ts:103-109`). None of those callers request `source`, `discord_channel_id`, or `is_hidden`, so the downstream badge, jump-link, and hide logic proposed in the plan would have no inputs unless the callers themselves are revised.
resolved_flags.resolution: Reworked v1 to address all 17 gate-identified defects. Largest structural changes: (1) promoter becomes a PL/pgSQL stored function invoked by pg_cron directly, matching the repo's existing `schedule_priority_scores_cron.sql` pattern and eliminating the edge-function auth problem; (2) discovery uses an anti-join on `assets.discord_thread_id` within a rolling 60-day window instead of a `created_at > cursor` scan, so late-threshold crossings are imported; (3) media importer reuses the existing `cloudflare-stream-webhook` + `cloudflare-stream-backfill` functions (no Cloudflare polling); (4) frontend work moves to `ResourceDetail/index.tsx` (the live surface) with matching changes at `ResourceModal.tsx` to honor the user's scope note; (5) asset `type` mapping fixed to `workflow`/`lora` matching `SubmitResource/index.tsx:10-13`; (6) member fallback removed — trust archive pipeline; (7) `useResources`, `useCommunityResources`, `useCommunityResource`, `useUserProfile` all updated to SELECT + filter on `is_hidden`; (8) `asset_comment_media` + `asset_media` gain `is_deleted` columns for attachment-removal-on-edit; (9) vault bootstrap for service-role key is a first-class migration step; (10) shared `AssetDescription` component applies markdown rendering to both manual and Discord-imported asset descriptions with an explicit success criterion, no custom embed tokens.

resolved_flags.concern: Find the callers of the changed function. What arguments do they actually pass? Does the fix handle all of them?: I also checked the direct callers of the UI surface the plan wants to edit. `rg -n "ResourceModal" banodoco-website/src` returns only the component definition and no imports/usages, which means there are currently no callers passing assets into that modal at all. The meaningful caller path today is the route-driven `ResourceDetail` page, so the proposed fix is landing at the wrong boundary unless the plan also restores or introduces actual `ResourceModal` usage.
resolved_flags.resolution: Reworked v1 to address all 17 gate-identified defects. Largest structural changes: (1) promoter becomes a PL/pgSQL stored function invoked by pg_cron directly, matching the repo's existing `schedule_priority_scores_cron.sql` pattern and eliminating the edge-function auth problem; (2) discovery uses an anti-join on `assets.discord_thread_id` within a rolling 60-day window instead of a `created_at > cursor` scan, so late-threshold crossings are imported; (3) media importer reuses the existing `cloudflare-stream-webhook` + `cloudflare-stream-backfill` functions (no Cloudflare polling); (4) frontend work moves to `ResourceDetail/index.tsx` (the live surface) with matching changes at `ResourceModal.tsx` to honor the user's scope note; (5) asset `type` mapping fixed to `workflow`/`lora` matching `SubmitResource/index.tsx:10-13`; (6) member fallback removed — trust archive pipeline; (7) `useResources`, `useCommunityResources`, `useCommunityResource`, `useUserProfile` all updated to SELECT + filter on `is_hidden`; (8) `asset_comment_media` + `asset_media` gain `is_deleted` columns for attachment-removal-on-edit; (9) vault bootstrap for service-role key is a first-class migration step; (10) shared `AssetDescription` component applies markdown rendering to both manual and Discord-imported asset descriptions with an explicit success criterion, no custom embed tokens.

signals.loop_summary: Iteration 2. Weighted score trajectory: 23.25 -> 20.75. Plan deltas: 91.5%. Recurring critiques: 1. Resolved flags: 9. Open significant flags: 13.

items.criterion: All Phase 1 migrations apply cleanly via `supabase db reset` on a scratch branch with no errors
`assets` gains source, discord_thread_id, discord_channel_id, imported_at, last_synced_at, reactions_reached_threshold_at, is_hidden plus unique partial index on discord_thread_id
`asset_comments`, `asset_comment_media` (with is_deleted), `media_import_jobs` exist with specified columns, indexes, and FKs to `members(member_id)` (not discord_members)
`asset_media` gains is_deleted BOOLEAN DEFAULT FALSE
RLS enabled on all new tables; asset_comments and asset_comment_media have public-read SELECT policies filtered by is_deleted=false; media_import_jobs has no policies (service-role only)
Vault bootstrap migration creates `internal.get_service_role_key()` SECURITY DEFINER helper; EXECUTE granted only to postgres
`internal.discord_promote_resources()` PL/pgSQL function exists, is SECURITY DEFINER, and is callable via pg_cron without HTTP

Running the promoter against a 60-day backfill produces assets for all qualifying OPs (reaction_count >= 5, message_id=thread_id) across all 5 channels, with type='workflow' for the `resources` channel and type='lora' for the four model-family channels
Running the promoter a second time produces zero net new rows in assets, asset_comments, or media_import_jobs (idempotency)
A previously-imported OP that later has an attachment removed via Discord edit results in the matching `asset_comment_media`/`asset_media` row getting is_deleted=true on the next promoter tick

A message_id=thread_id row older than the 10-min polling window that has just crossed reaction_count=5 (simulated by manually setting the count) is imported on the next promoter tick (late-threshold regression test for FLAG-001)
A Discord message edit updates asset_comments.content and discord_edited_at; a Discord deletion (discord_messages.is_deleted=true) sets asset_comments.is_deleted=true

Media importer, given a single seeded job, creates a media row (with Stream UID for video; with public Storage URL for image), creates the matching asset_media or asset_comment_media junction row, and marks the job status='done' with media_id set
For a video attachment: the importer does not poll Cloudflare; the existing cloudflare-stream-webhook populates the media row's HLS URL / thumbnail / duration when the Stream asset becomes ready
Rehosted video URLs return a valid HLS playlist (HTTP 200 with `#EXTM3U` in body); rehosted image URLs return HTTP 200 with an image content-type
A simulated failed job increments attempts, records last_error, is re-queued with growing locked_until, and moves to status='failed' after 5 attempts

pg_cron has two schedules: `discord-resource-promoter` (*/10, calls internal.discord_promote_resources() directly) and `discord-media-importer` (*/2, calls the edge function via net.http_post with Authorization from internal.get_service_role_key())
useResources.ts, useCommunityResources.ts, useCommunityResource.ts, useUserProfile.ts all SELECT source, discord_channel_id, is_hidden and filter is_hidden=false

A shared AssetDescription component (banodoco-website/src/components/resources/AssetDescription.tsx) renders asset.description using react-markdown + remark-gfm mirroring PostBodyRenderer.tsx, with NO custom embed tokens (::art[uuid], etc.)
ResourceDetail/index.tsx renders asset.description via AssetDescription for both manual and Discord-imported assets (the plain-text block at lines 166-169 is replaced)

ResourceModal.tsx renders asset.description via AssetDescription (the whitespace-pre-line

at lines 186-189 is replaced)
ResourceDetail shows 'Made with this' grid, 'Discussion' thread, and 'From Discord' badge only when resource.source === 'discord_import'; manual assets render unchanged

Each rendered comment shows author avatar + name from members, markdown-rendered content via AssetDescription, reply chip when applicable, inline attachment thumbnails with working lightbox, reaction count chip, and a working 'View on Discord' link
useAssetComments fetches the joined data in a single Supabase query, filters is_deleted=false on both asset_comments and asset_comment_media, and orders by discord_created_at ascending

Setting assets.is_hidden=true on an imported asset makes it disappear from all four hook-backed surfaces (useResources, useCommunityResources, useCommunityResource, useUserProfile) and from ResourceDetail if accessed by URL
Existing website tests and Supabase type generation still pass after schema additions
Resource cards show a subtle 'From Discord' badge with the channel name for imported assets
Media importer wall-clock stays under 400s per invocation; promoter (SQL) finishes in under 10s per run — observed across several cron cycles
Health-check SQL snippets return usable output (stuck jobs, 24h failures, staleness) against live tables
Per-phase rollback documentation accurately reflects the migrations, cron jobs, edge function, and vault bootstrap introduced
No Cloudflare Stream quota / storage cost surprises during 60-day backfill

unresolved_flags.concern: Discord resource promotion: late-threshold coverage is still incomplete because the revised rolling 60-day scan misses threads older than 60 days that reach the reaction threshold later.
unresolved_flags.evidence: The revised plan replaces the created_at cursor with `created_at > NOW() - INTERVAL '60 days'`, but `discord_messages.reaction_count` is still updated after message creation by the trigger in `supabase/migrations/20260319100000_fix_sync_reactors_trigger.sql:1-22`.

unresolved_flags.concern: Discord media sync infrastructure: the revised Cloudflare/vault reuse story is still incomplete against the actual repository contracts.
unresolved_flags.evidence: `supabase/functions/cloudflare-stream-webhook/index.ts:210-240` updates HLS URL and thumbnail but not duration, while the revised plan says the existing webhook will populate duration too; the repo also has no existing `vault.create_secret` or `internal.get_service_role_key` pattern under `supabase/`.

unresolved_flags.concern: Are the proposed changes technically correct?: The replacement for FLAG-001 is still incomplete. V2 no longer uses `created_at > cursor`, but it still limits discovery to `discord_messages.created_at > NOW() - INTERVAL '60 days'`; a thread created 61+ days ago that only later reaches 5 reactions would still never import, even though the product rule says any qualifying OP becomes an asset and the ongoing sync is not scoped to recent posts only.
unresolved_flags.evidence: The replacement for FLAG-001 is still incomplete. V2 no longer uses `created_at > cursor`, but it still limits discovery to `discord_messages.created_at > NOW() - INTERVAL '60 days'`; a thread created 61+ days ago that only later reaches 5 reactions would still never import, even though the product rule says any qualifying OP becomes an asset and the ongoing sync is not scoped to recent posts only.

unresolved_flags.concern: Are the proposed changes technically correct?: The plan’s failure-handling story for missing members is not technically sufficient in SQL form. It says an FK miss will be 'logged and skipped', but the proposed promoter logic is a set-based `INSERT ... SELECT` inside one PL/pgSQL function; if one row violates the `members(member_id)` FK, PostgreSQL aborts the statement unless the plan introduces row-level exception isolation, so one bad author can block the whole promoter run rather than just one asset/comment.
unresolved_flags.evidence: The plan’s failure-handling story for missing members is not technically sufficient in SQL form. It says an FK miss will be 'logged and skipped', but the proposed promoter logic is a set-based `INSERT ... SELECT` inside one PL/pgSQL function; if one row violates the `members(member_id)` FK, PostgreSQL aborts the statement unless the plan introduces row-level exception isolation, so one bad author can block the whole promoter run rather than just one asset/comment.

unresolved_flags.concern: Are the proposed changes technically correct?: The proposed asset type mapping (`lora` or `tool`) does not match the website’s current resource model. Manual resource creation only offers `lora` and `workflow` (`banodoco-website/src/pages/SubmitResource/index.tsx:10-13`), and the cards/filters branch on `asset.type === 'workflow'` (`ResourceModal.tsx:151-156`, `ResourceCard.tsx:151-154`, `useResourceFilters.ts:159-160`). Importing Discord rows with `type='tool'` would render them as LoRAs in some places and make them unfilterable in others.
unresolved_flags.evidence: Reworked v1 to address all 17 gate-identified defects. Largest structural changes: (1) promoter becomes a PL/pgSQL stored function invoked by pg_cron directly, matching the repo's existing `schedule_priority_scores_cron.sql` pattern and eliminating the edge-function auth problem; (2) discovery uses an anti-join on `assets.discord_thread_id` within a rolling 60-day window instead of a `created_at > cursor` scan, so late-threshold crossings are imported; (3) media importer reuses the existing `cloudflare-stream-webhook` + `cloudflare-stream-backfill` functions (no Cloudflare polling); (4) frontend work moves to `ResourceDetail/index.tsx` (the live surface) with matching changes at `ResourceModal.tsx` to honor the user's scope note; (5) asset `type` mapping fixed to `workflow`/`lora` matching `SubmitResource/index.tsx:10-13`; (6) member fallback removed — trust archive pipeline; (7) `useResources`, `useCommunityResources`, `useCommunityResource`, `useUserProfile` all updated to SELECT + filter on `is_hidden`; (8) `asset_comment_media` + `asset_media` gain `is_deleted` columns for attachment-removal-on-edit; (9) vault bootstrap for service-role key is a first-class migration step; (10) shared `AssetDescription` component applies markdown rendering to both manual and Discord-imported asset descriptions with an explicit success criterion, no custom embed tokens.

unresolved_flags.concern: Does the change touch all locations AND supporting infrastructure?: The Cloudflare reuse story is still incomplete against the actual repository code. `supabase/functions/cloudflare-stream-webhook/index.ts:210-240` only writes `cloudflare_playback_hls_url`, `cloudflare_thumbnail_url`, and `storage_provider`; it does not persist duration, even though the revised plan and success criteria say the existing webhook will populate HLS, thumbnail, and duration for imported videos. That supporting-infrastructure contract is still inaccurate unless the plan adds a webhook change or drops duration from the promise.
unresolved_flags.evidence: The Cloudflare reuse story is still incomplete against the actual repository code. `supabase/functions/cloudflare-stream-webhook/index.ts:210-240` only writes `cloudflare_playback_hls_url`, `cloudflare_thumbnail_url`, and `storage_provider`; it does not persist duration, even though the revised plan and success criteria say the existing webhook will populate HLS, thumbnail, and duration for imported videos. That supporting-infrastructure contract is still inaccurate unless the plan adds a webhook change or drops duration from the promise.

unresolved_flags.concern: Does the change touch all locations AND supporting infrastructure?: The new vault bootstrap step is not yet self-contained enough to satisfy the plan’s own `supabase db reset` criterion. There is no existing `vault.create_secret`, `internal.get_service_role_key`, or secret-seeding pattern anywhere under `supabase/`, and the proposed migration depends on an out-of-band `current_setting('app.settings.service_role_key_value', true)` value that the repo never sets. As written, the bootstrap is still an infrastructure gap rather than a ready migration recipe.
unresolved_flags.evidence: The new vault bootstrap step is not yet self-contained enough to satisfy the plan’s own `supabase db reset` criterion. There is no existing `vault.create_secret`, `internal.get_service_role_key`, or secret-seeding pattern anywhere under `supabase/`, and the proposed migration depends on an out-of-band `current_setting('app.settings.service_role_key_value', true)` value that the repo never sets. As written, the bootstrap is still an infrastructure gap rather than a ready migration recipe.

unresolved_flags.concern: Does the change touch all locations AND supporting infrastructure?: The cron registration snippet assumes a secret-delivery pattern that is not present elsewhere in the repo. Existing pg_cron usage schedules local SQL directly (`supabase/migrations/20260403000005_schedule_priority_scores_cron.sql:4-23`), and `supabase/config.toml:45-46` only contains a commented `db.vault` stub. A migration that references `current_setting('app.settings.service_role_key')` without first establishing that setting will not be self-contained.

unresolved_flags.concern: Discord promoter execution: the revised SQL-native promoter still lacks row-level error isolation, so one missing-member FK or other bad row can abort the whole sync instead of being logged and skipped.
unresolved_flags.evidence: The plan's promoter remains a set-based `INSERT ... SELECT` inside one PL/pgSQL function, but PostgreSQL aborts the statement on FK violation unless the implementation adds per-row exception handling or prefilters invalid rows.

unresolved_flags.concern: Discord resource discussion UI: the revised frontend plan still assumes an existing comment lightbox/navigation surface that does not exist in the current caller path.
unresolved_flags.evidence: `banodoco-website/src/pages/ResourceDetail/index.tsx:59-74` only manages inline `activeMedia` state, and `rg -n "ResourceModal" banodoco-website/src` still shows no callers for the modal component.

unresolved_flags.concern: Did the work fully address the issue hints, user notes, and approved plan requirements?: The revision diverges from the user’s stated runtime/deliverable shape in a way that still needs to be flagged. The brief asked for two Supabase Edge Functions plus pg_cron, with a promoter function directory under `supabase/functions/`, and a resumable per-channel cursor table; v2 replaces the promoter with PL/pgSQL and removes the cursor table entirely. That may be a pragmatic simplification, but it is still a requirements change rather than a straight implementation of the approved shape.
